body: PT Mitra Adiperkasa Tbk (MAPI) reported a net profit of IDR 1.1 trillion for the first nine months of 2024, a 7.3% year-over-year increase, driven by a 29.6% growth in net income to IDR 12.4 trillion. The company's solid growth was supported by a positive market response during the back-to-school season and high demand for sports and kids' leisure products, as well as its effective multi-channel business model and digital strategy.
title: MAPA Subsidiary Posts 7.3% Increase in Net Profit to IDR 1.1 Trillion in First Nine Months of 2024.

body: PT Merdeka Battery Materials Tbk (MBMA) plans to issue 10.79 billion new shares, equivalent to 10% of its issued and paid-up capital, through a private placement to raise funds for working capital and business development. The company will seek shareholder approval on November 13, 2024, and aims to implement the private placement within 2 years of receiving permission.
title: Merdeka Battery Materials to issue 10.79 billion new shares to raise capital for working capital and business development.

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) recorded a 5.7% year-on-year credit growth at the end of September 2024, with home ownership credit growing higher than the industry average at 13% year-on-year. The bank's consumer credit sector also grew 15% year-on-year, driven by various stimuli in the property sector, and BNI aims to achieve 10-12% credit growth by the end of 2024.
title: BNI Records Moderate Credit Growth of 5.7% Year-on-Year, Driven by Strong Home Ownership Credit Segment.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange recorded 10 top gainers with share price growth ranging from 98% to 284% from August to September 2024, with PT Multipolar Technology Tbk (MLPT) leading the pack with a 284.97% increase to IDR 6,275 per share. Other notable gainers include PT Sona Topas Tourism Industry Tbk (SONA) with a 210.71% increase, PT Perdana Karya Perkasa Tbk (PKPK) with a 158% increase, and PT Aesler Grup Internasional Tbk (ASLE) with a 140.58% increase.
title: Indonesian Stock Exchange Sees 10 Top Gainers with Share Price Growth Ranging from 98% to 284% in August-September 2024.

body: The Indonesian government is urged to be cautious in its plan to save PT Sri Rejeki Isman Tbk (Sritex) and consider alternative forms of assistance beyond monetary aid, as the company's bankruptcy process is already underway. The government's potential rescue plan should be carefully structured, as seen in the case of Jiwasraya, where the state is a shareholder, unlike Sritex, and the company will undergo internal and external consolidation to benefit stakeholders.
title: Indonesian Government Urged to Explore Alternative Forms of Assistance to Save PT Sri Rejeki Isman TBK.
